rationale: Both correctly identify the ground truth's actual resolution (run-level misconfiguration causing dummy weights, closed invalid) and cite the same core evidence (stage_config.py's dummy load_format patching, run_args.py default, PR #4354, and akshatvishu's debug log). X grounds more broadly (adds runtime.py fixture logic, assertions.py thresholds, and explicitly explains why selecting the test by node ID doesn't override run-level despite the full_model marker — a subtlety Y leaves implicit). X is also more end-to-end, adding concrete pass requirements and a constructive footgun/ergonomics suggestion, whereas Y's answer is solid but narrower and includes stray tool-output boilerplate ('Run report' header) that reads as less polished.
